title: GitHub - fthiella/filtersql: filtersql library for AI, Datatables and frontend applications
excerpt: filtersql library for AI, Datatables and frontend applications - fthiella/filtersql
content_html:
Frontends, REST APIs and LLMs naturally produce structured JSON - not SQL. filtersql defines a declarative JSON query language and compiles it into safe, parameterized SQL.

JSON payload (or Python dicts) → filtersql → SQL string + values list

It is intentionally not an ORM or a connection manager. It builds the query and returns it. Query execution remains the responsibility of the caller, making filtersql compatible with any Python DB driver.

Designed for three use cases:

  • DataTables server-side backends
  • Cursor-based pagination (Access-style, no OFFSET)
  • Deterministic LLM pipelines - the LLM generates JSON, filtersql compiles it to safe SQL

Supports PostgreSQL, SQLite, MySQL, and Oracle.

Features

  • Secure by default - Fully parameterized queries, protected against SQL injection
  • Multi-database support - PostgreSQL, SQLite, MySQL, and Oracle
  • Lightweight - No ORM required. Works with any database driver (psycopg2, sqlite3, mysql-connector, cx_Oracle, etc.)
  • Language-agnostic - Clean JSON protocol, perfect for REST APIs and frontend applications
  • High-performance pagination - Keyset (cursor-based) pagination, avoiding slow OFFSET queries
  • AI/LLM friendly - Designed for structured output from large language models

Why filtersql?

filtersql is intentionally:

  • stateless
  • deterministic
  • driver agnostic
  • parameterized
  • declarative
  • portable
  • JSON-first
  • LLM friendly

Applications describe what they want. filtersql decides how to express it in SQL.

Example

From JSON:

{
  "action": "select",
  "source": "users",
  "filters": [
    {
      "field": "name",
      "operator": "icontains",
      "value": "john"
    }
  ]
}

to SQL:

select
  *
from
  "users"
where
  "name" ilike '%' || ? || '%'

with values:

["john"]

Install

pip install filtersql

Quick start

import filtersql

ds = filtersql.Datasource(
    source      = 'users',
    dbms        = 'Pg',
    placeholder = '%s',
)

query, values = ds.select(
    columns = [
        {'field': 'id'},
        {'field': 'first_name'},
        {'field': 'last_name'},
    ],
    filters = [
        {'field': 'first_name', 'operator': 'icontains', 'value': 'John'},
        {'field': 'last_name',  'operator': 'icontains', 'value': 'Smith'},
    ],
    order = [{'field': 'first_name', 'order': 'asc'}],
    limit = {'start': 0, 'length': 10},
)

print(query)
# select
#   "id",
#   "first_name",
#   "last_name"
# from
#   "users"
# where
#   ("first_name" ilike '%' || %s || '%' and "last_name" ilike '%' || %s || '%')
# order by
#   "first_name" asc
# limit 10 offset 0

print(values)
# ['John', 'Smith']

cursor.execute(query, values)
rows = cursor.fetchall()

Filters

Filter format

Every filter is a dict with three keys:

{'field': 'status', 'operator': '=', 'value': 'active'}
  • field - column name, JSONB path (attributes->>amount), or schema-prefixed (m.doc_type)
  • operator - the comparison operator (see table below)
  • value - the value to compare against

A flat list of filters is joined with AND:

filters = [
    {'field': 'status',   'operator': '=',         'value': 'active'},
    {'field': 'doc_date', 'operator': '>=',        'value': '2025-01-01'},
    {'field': 'title',    'operator': 'icontains', 'value': 'oxygen'},
]

Some operators don't need a value:

{'field': 'deleted_at', 'operator': 'null'}
{'field': 'deleted_at', 'operator': 'notnull'}

Some operators take a list:

{'field': 'status',   'operator': 'in',      'value': ['active', 'pending']}
{'field': 'doc_date', 'operator': 'between', 'value': ['2025-01-01', '2025-12-31']}

OR groups

Wrap filters in a dict with key 'or':

filters = [
    {'field': 'status', 'operator': '=', 'value': 'active'},
    {'or': [
        {'field': 'first_name', 'operator': 'icontains', 'value': 'john'},
        {'field': 'last_name',  'operator': 'icontains', 'value': 'john'},
    ]},
]
# → "status" = ? AND ("first_name" like '%'||?||'%' OR "last_name" like '%'||?||'%')

Nesting

OR groups can contain AND groups and vice versa:

filters = [
    {'or': [
        {'field': 'doc_type', 'operator': '=', 'value': 'CONTRACT'},
        {'and': [
            {'field': 'doc_type', 'operator': '=',  'value': 'ORDER'},
            {'field': 'amount',   'operator': '>=', 'value': '10000'},
        ]},
    ]},
]
# → ("doc_type" = ? OR ("doc_type" = ? AND "amount" >= ?))

scope

scope is a dict of fixed field: value pairs applied as = filters to every operation on the Datasource - select, where, update, delete, and insert.

ds = filtersql.Datasource(
    source      = 'documents',
    dbms        = 'Pg',
    placeholder = '%s',
    scope       = {'tenant_id': 42},
)

# scope is always added
query, values = ds.select(columns=columns, filters=user_filters)
# → ... WHERE "tenant_id" = %s AND ...

query, values = ds.delete(id={'id': 99})
# → ... WHERE "id" = %s AND "tenant_id" = %s

query, values = ds.insert(values={'title': 'New doc'})
# → INSERT INTO "documents" ("title", "tenant_id") VALUES (%s, %s)

Columns

Columns are passed per call to select(), not at construction time - they can be computed at runtime.

Each column is a dict with a field key. Any extra keys (label, visible, editable etc.) are ignored by filtersql and can be used by your frontend layer:

columns = [
    {'field': 'id',         'label': 'ID',         'visible': True,  'editable': False},
    {'field': 'first_name', 'label': 'First Name', 'visible': True,  'editable': True},
    {'field': 'last_name',  'label': 'Last Name',  'visible': True,  'editable': True},
]

Plain strings also work:

columns = ['id', 'first_name', 'last_name']

Raw expressions with raw=True:

columns = [
    {'field': 'COUNT(*) as total', 'raw': True},
    {'field': 'MAX(age) as max_age', 'raw': True},
]

Insert, update, delete

All three return (query, values) like select().

# insert
query, values = ds.insert(
    values = {'first_name': 'John', 'last_name': 'Smith'},
)

# insert with returning (PostgreSQL only)
query, values = ds.insert(
    values    = {'first_name': 'John'},
    returning = 'id',
)

# update - id can be single or composite key
query, values = ds.update(
    id     = {'id': 42},
    values = {'first_name': 'John', 'status': 'active'},
)
# values order: SET values first, then WHERE values
# → ['John', 'active', 42]

# delete
query, values = ds.delete(id={'id': 42})

where() - just the WHERE clause

When you want to inject filters into your own handwritten query:

ds = filtersql.Datasource(source='documents', dbms='Pg', placeholder='%s')

where_clause, values = ds.where(filters=[
    {'field': 'doc_type', 'operator': '=',  'value': 'CONTRACT'},
    {'field': 'doc_date', 'operator': '>=', 'value': '2025-01-01'},
])
# → '("doc_type" = %s and "doc_date" >= %s)'
# → ['CONTRACT', '2025-01-01']

query = f"""
    SELECT v.chunk, m.doc_type
    FROM file_vectors v
    JOIN file_metadata m ON v.sha256 = m.sha256
    WHERE v.model_name = %s
    AND {where_clause}
    ORDER BY v.embedding <=> %s
"""

filtersql() - convenience function

Builds any query from a single payload dict. Useful for REST APIs and AI-generated actions:

from filtersql import filtersql

query, values = filtersql(
    payload = {
        'action':  'select',
        'source':  'users',
        'columns': [{'field': 'id'}, {'field': 'first_name'}],
        'filters': [{'field': 'status', 'operator': '=', 'value': 'active'}],
        'order':   [{'field': 'id', 'order': 'asc'}],
        'limit':   {'start': 0, 'length': 10},
    },
    dbms        = 'Pg',
    placeholder = '%s',
)

Supported actions: select, insert, update, delete.

debug()

Replaces placeholders with actual values for logging. Never use the output as real SQL.

query, values = ds.select(columns=columns, filters=filters)
print(ds.debug(query, values))
# select
#   "id",
#   "first_name"
# from
#   "users"
# where
#   ("first_name" ilike '%' || 'John' || '%')

AI / LLM pipelines

LLMs should never generate SQL directly. They should generate structured intent. filtersql validates that intent and compiles it into parameterized SQL. The values are always parameterized - no SQL injection risk even if the model produces unexpected output.

user question → LLM → JSON → filtersql → parameterized SQL

# DANGEROUS!
# the naive approach everyone starts with
query = f"SELECT * FROM users WHERE {llm_output}"  # SQL injection waiting to happen

# with filtersql
payload = json.loads(llm_response)
query, values = filtersql(payload, dbms='Pg', placeholder='%s')
cursor.execute(query, values)  # always parameterized, always safe

The simplest version without Pydantic:

import json
from google import genai
from filtersql import filtersql

SCHEMA = {
    "type": "OBJECT",
    "properties": {
        "source": {"type": "STRING", "enum": ["users", "contracts"]},
        "filters": {
            "type": "ARRAY",
            "items": {
                "type": "OBJECT",
                "properties": {
                    "field":    {"type": "STRING"},
                    "operator": {"type": "STRING", "enum": ["=", "!=", ">", ">=", "<", "<=", "icontains", "in"]},
                    "value":    {"type": "STRING"}
                },
                "required": ["field", "operator", "value"]
            }
        }
    },
    "required": ["source", "filters"]
}

response = client.models.generate_content(
    model='gemini-flash',
    contents=user_question,
    config=genai.types.GenerateContentConfig(
        system_instruction="Convert user questions into SQL filter payloads.",
        response_mime_type="application/json",
        response_schema=SCHEMA,
        temperature=0.0,
    )
)

payload = json.loads(response.text)
query, values = filtersql(payload, action='select', dbms='SQLite', placeholder='?')

With Pydantic for stricter validation:

from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from typing import List, Literal

class SQLFilter(BaseModel):
    field: Literal['doc_type', 'doc_date', 'author', 'title']
    operator: Literal['=', '!=', '>', '>=', '<', '<=', 'icontains', 'between', 'in', 'notin']
    value: str

class QuerySchema(BaseModel):
    semantic_query: str
    sql_filters: List[SQLFilter]

parsed = QuerySchema.model_validate_json(response.text)
filters = [f.model_dump() for f in parsed.sql_filters]

ds = filtersql.Datasource(source='documents', dbms='Pg', placeholder='%s')
where_clause, values = ds.where(filters=filters)

JSONB and value_type (PostgreSQL)

{'field': 'attributes->>amount',  'operator': '>=', 'value': '10000',     'value_type': 'numeric'}
# → ("attributes"->>'amount')::numeric >= %s::numeric

{'field': 'attributes->>expiry',  'operator': '<=', 'value': '2025-12-31', 'value_type': 'date'}
# → ("attributes"->>'expiry')::date <= %s::date

Without the cast, '9' > '10' as text gives wrong results for numeric ranges.

Cursor-based pagination

Navigates large tables without OFFSET - no performance degradation.

ds = filtersql.Datasource(
    source = 'documents',
    dbms   = 'Pg',
    order  = [{'field': 'id', 'order': 'asc'}],
)

# forward
query, values = ds.select(
    columns   = columns,
    cursor    = {'id': last_seen_id},
    direction = 'next',
)

# backward
query, values = ds.select(
    columns   = columns,
    cursor    = {'id': last_seen_id},
    direction = 'prev',
)

# jump to specific record
query, values = ds.select(
    columns   = columns,
    cursor    = {'id': 42},
    direction = 'seek',
)
direction SQL Use case
'next' field > last_value Forward navigation
'prev' field < last_value Backward navigation
'seek' field = value Jump to specific record

Multi-column cursors work too:

query, values = ds.select(
    columns   = columns,
    cursor    = {'first_name': last_first, 'last_name': last_last},
    direction = 'next',
)

Full-text search

PostgreSQL

# pre-built tsvector column
{'field': 'tsv_content', 'operator': 'fts', 'value': 'oxygen supply'}
# → "tsv_content" @@ websearch_to_tsquery('english', %s)

# on the fly from a text column
{'field': 'description', 'operator': 'fts_query', 'value': 'oxygen supply'}
# → to_tsvector('english', coalesce("description", '')) @@ websearch_to_tsquery('english', %s)

ds = filtersql.Datasource(..., fts_language='italian')

MySQL

{'field': 'content', 'operator': 'fts', 'value': 'oxygen supply'}
# → match("content") against(? in boolean mode)

Filter operators

Operator Description
= != > >= < <= Comparison
between Range - value must be [low, high]
contains Case-sensitive substring
starts_with Case-sensitive prefix
ends_with Case-sensitive suffix
not_contains Case-sensitive substring exclusion
not_starts_with Case-sensitive prefix exclusion
icontains Case-insensitive substring
istarts_with Case-insensitive prefix
iends_with Case-insensitive suffix
not_icontains Case-insensitive substring exclusion
null / notnull NULL checks - no value needed
in / notin List membership
reverse_in Value in a set of columns - ? IN (col1, col2)
regexp Case-sensitive regular expression
iregexp Case-insensitive regexp (Pg: ~*, Oracle: regexp_like with i)
fts Full-text search on indexed column (Pg, MySQL)
fts_query Full-text search on text column (Pg only)

Supported databases

DBMS dbms value Default placeholder
PostgreSQL 'Pg' %s
SQLite 'SQLite' ?
MySQL 'mysql' ?
Oracle 'Oracle' ?

Override with any placeholder your driver expects:

ds = filtersql.Datasource(..., placeholder='%s')
ds = filtersql.Datasource(..., placeholder=':val')

Examples

Working examples are in the /examples folder:

  • examples/datatables/ - Flask + DataTables server-side with column filters and global search
  • examples/pagination/ - REST API gateway with security whitelist for insert/update/delete
  • examples/ai/ - Gemini structured output → filtersql → SQLite

License

MIT

column name, JSONB path (attributes->>amount), or schema-prefixed (m.doc_type) operator - the comparison operator (see table below) value - the value to compare against A flat list of filters is joined with AND: filters = [ {'field': 'status', 'operator': '=', 'value': 'active'}, {'field': 'doc_date', 'operator': '>=', 'value': '2025-01-01'}, {'field': 'title', 'operator': 'icontains', 'value': 'oxygen'}, ] Some operators don't need a value: {'field': 'deleted_at', 'operator': 'null'} {'field': 'deleted_at', 'operator': 'notnull'} Some operators take a list: {'field': 'status', 'operator': 'in', 'value': ['active', 'pending']} {'field': 'doc_date', 'operator': 'between', 'value': ['2025-01-01', '2025-12-31']} OR groups Wrap filters in a dict with key 'or': filters = [ {'field': 'status', 'operator': '=', 'value': 'active'}, {'or': [ {'field': 'first_name', 'operator': 'icontains', 'value': 'john'}, {'field': 'last_name', 'operator': 'icontains', 'value': 'john'}, ]}, ] # → "status" = ? AND ("first_name" like '%'||?||'%' OR "last_name" like '%'||?||'%') Nesting OR groups can contain AND groups and vice versa: filters = [ {'or': [ {'field': 'doc_type', 'operator': '=', 'value': 'CONTRACT'}, {'and': [ {'field': 'doc_type', 'operator': '=', 'value': 'ORDER'}, {'field': 'amount', 'operator': '>=', 'value': '10000'}, ]}, ]}, ] # → ("doc_type" = ? OR ("doc_type" = ? WHERE "tenant_id" = %s AND ... query, values = ds.delete(id={'id': 99}) # → ... WHERE "id" = %s AND "tenant_id" = %s query, values = ds.insert(values={'title': 'New doc'}) # → INSERT INTO "documents" ("title", "tenant_id") VALUES (%s, %s) Columns Columns are passed per call to select(), not at construction time - they can be computed at runtime. Each column is a dict with a field key. Any extra keys (label, visible, editable etc.) are ignored by filtersql and can be used by your frontend layer: columns = [ {'field': 'id', 'label': 'ID', 'visible': True, 'editable': False}, {'field': 'first_name', 'label': 'First Name', 'visible': True, 'editable': True}, {'field': 'last_name', 'label': 'Last Name', 'visible': True, 'editable': True}, ] Plain strings also work: columns = ['id', 'first_name', 'last_name'] Raw expressions with raw=True: columns = [ {'field': 'COUNT(*) as total', 'raw': True}, {'field': 'MAX(age) as max_age', 'raw': True}, ] Insert, update, delete All three return (query, values) like select(). # insert query, values = ds.insert( values = {'first_name': 'John', 'last_name': 'Smith'}, ) # insert with returning (PostgreSQL only) query, values = ds.insert( values = {'first_name': 'John'}, returning = 'id', ) # update - id can be single or composite key query, values = ds.update( id = {'id': 42}, values = {'first_name': 'John', 'status': 'active'}, ) # values order: SET values first, then WHERE values # → ['John', 'active', 42] # delete query, values = ds.delete(id={'id': 42}) where() - just the WHERE clause When you want to inject filters into your own handwritten query: ds = filtersql.Datasource(source='documents', dbms='Pg', placeholder='%s') where_clause, values = ds.where(filters=[ {'field': 'doc_type', 'operator': '=', 'value': 'CONTRACT'}, {'field': 'doc_date', 'operator': '>=', 'value': '2025-01-01'}, ]) # → '("doc_type" = %s and "doc_date" >= %s)' # → ['CONTRACT', '2025-01-01'] query = f""" SELECT v.chunk, m.doc_type FROM file_vectors v JOIN file_metadata m ON v.sha256 = m.sha256 WHERE v.model_name = %s AND {where_clause} ORDER BY v.embedding <=> %s """ filtersql() - convenience function Builds any query from a single payload dict. Cursor-based pagination Navigates large tables without OFFSET - no performance degradation. ds = filtersql.Datasource( source = 'documents', dbms = 'Pg', order = [{'field': 'id', 'order': 'asc'}], ) # forward query, values = ds.select( columns = columns, cursor = {'id': last_seen_id}, direction = 'next', ) # backward query, values = ds.select( columns = columns, cursor = {'id': last_seen_id}, direction = 'prev', ) # jump to specific record query, values = ds.select( columns = columns, cursor = {'id': 42}, direction = 'seek', ) direction SQL Use case 'next' field > last_value Forward navigation 'prev' field < last_value Backward navigation 'seek' field = value Jump to specific record Multi-column cursors work too: query, values = ds.select( columns = columns, cursor = {'first_name': last_first, 'last_name': last_last}, direction = 'next', ) Full-text search PostgreSQL # pre-built tsvector column {'field': 'tsv_content', 'operator': 'fts', 'value': 'oxygen supply'} # → "tsv_content" @@ websearch_to_tsquery('english', %s) # on the fly from a text column {'field': 'description', 'operator': 'fts_query', 'value': 'oxygen supply'} # → to_tsvector('english', coalesce("description", '')) @@ websearch_to_tsquery('english', %s) ds = filtersql.Datasource(..., fts_language='italian') MySQL {'field': 'content', 'operator': 'fts', 'value': 'oxygen supply'} # → match("content") against(? in boolean mode) Filter operators Operator Description = != > >= < <= Comparison between Range - value must be [low, high] contains Case-sensitive substring starts_with Case-sensitive prefix ends_with Case-sensitive suffix not_contains Case-sensitive substring exclusion not_starts_with Case-sensitive prefix exclusion icontains Case-insensitive substring istarts_with Case-insensitive prefix iends_with Case-insensitive suffix not_icontains Case-insensitive substring exclusion null / notnull NULL checks - no value needed in / notin List membership reverse_in Value in a set of columns - ? IN (col1, col2) regexp Case-sensitive regular expression iregexp Case-insensitive regexp (Pg: ~*, Oracle: regexp_like with i) fts Full-text search on indexed column (Pg, MySQL) fts_query Full-text search on text column (Pg only) Supported databases DBMS dbms value Default placeholder PostgreSQL 'Pg' %s SQLite 'SQLite' ? MySQL 'mysql' ? Oracle 'Oracle' ? Override with any placeholder your driver expects: ds = filtersql.Datasource(..., placeholder='%s') ds = filtersql.Datasource(..., placeholder=':val') Examples Working examples are in the /examples folder: examples/datatables/ - Flask + DataTables server-side with column filters and global search examples/pagination/ - REST API gateway with security whitelist for insert/update/delete examples/ai/ - Gemini structured output → filtersql → SQLite License MIT

blocks.html_en: Frontends, REST APIs and LLMs naturally produce structured JSON - not SQL. filtersql defines a declarative JSON query language and compiles it into safe, parameterized SQL.

sentences.en: Frontends, REST APIs and LLMs naturally produce structured JSON - not SQL.
sentences.zh: 前端、REST API 和 LLM 自然会生成结构化 JSON，而不是 SQL。

sentences.en: filtersql defines a declarative JSON query language and compiles it into safe, parameterized SQL.
sentences.zh: filtersql 定义了一种声明式 JSON 查询语言，并将其编译为安全的参数化 SQL。

blocks.html_en: It is intentionally not an ORM or a connection manager. It builds the query and returns it. Query execution remains the responsibility of the caller, making filtersql compatible with any Python DB driver.

sentences.en: It is intentionally not an ORM or a connection manager.
sentences.zh: 它故意不是 ORM 或连接管理器。

sentences.en: It builds the query and returns it.
Query execution remains the responsibility of the caller, making filtersql compatible with any Python DB driver.
sentences.zh: 查询执行仍然由调用者负责，这使得filtersql与任何Python DB驱动程序兼容。
基于光标的分页（Access 样式，无 OFFSET）

blocks.html_en: • Deterministic LLM pipelines - the LLM generates JSON, filtersql compiles it to safe SQL

sentences.en: Deterministic LLM pipelines - the LLM generates JSON, filtersql compiles it to safe SQL
sentences.zh: 确定性 LLM 管道 - LLM 生成 JSON，filtersql 将其编译为安全 SQL

blocks.html_en: Supports PostgreSQL, SQLite, MySQL, and Oracle.

sentences.en: Supports PostgreSQL, SQLite, MySQL, and Oracle.

blocks.html_en: • Secure by default - Fully parameterized queries, protected against SQL injection

sentences.en: Secure by default - Fully parameterized queries, protected against SQL injection
sentences.zh: 默认安全 - 完全参数化查询，防止 SQL 注入

blocks.html_en: • Multi-database support - PostgreSQL, SQLite, MySQL, and Oracle

sentences.en: Multi-database support - PostgreSQL, SQLite, MySQL, and Oracle
sentences.zh: 多数据库支持 - PostgreSQL、SQLite、MySQL 和 Oracle

blocks.html_en: • Lightweight - No ORM required. Works with any database driver (psycopg2, sqlite3, mysql-connector, cx_Oracle, etc.)

sentences.zh: 轻量级 - 不需要 ORM。

sentences.en: Works with any database driver (psycopg2, sqlite3, mysql-connector, cx_Oracle, etc.)
sentences.zh: 适用于任何数据库驱动程序（psycopg2、sqlite3、mysql-connector、cx_Oracle 等）

blocks.html_en: • Language-agnostic - Clean JSON protocol, perfect for REST APIs and frontend applications

sentences.en: Language-agnostic - Clean JSON protocol, perfect for REST APIs and frontend applications
sentences.zh: 与语言无关 - 干净的 JSON 协议，非常适合 REST API 和前端应用程序

blocks.html_en: • High-performance pagination - Keyset (cursor-based) pagination, avoiding slow OFFSET queries

sentences.en: High-performance pagination - Keyset (cursor-based) pagination, avoiding slow OFFSET queries
sentences.zh: 高性能分页 - 键集（基于光标）分页，避免缓慢的 OFFSET 查询

blocks.html_en: • AI/LLM friendly - Designed for structured output from large language models

sentences.en: AI/LLM friendly - Designed for structured output from large language models
sentences.zh: AI/LLM 友好 - 专为大型语言模型的结构化输出而设计

blocks.html_en: Applications describe what they want. filtersql decides how to express it in SQL.

sentences.zh: 应用程序描述了他们想要什么。

sentences.en: filtersql decides how to express it in SQL.

blocks.html_en: Every filter is a dict with three keys:

sentences.en: Every filter is a dict with three keys:
sentences.zh: 每个过滤器都是一个具有三个键的字典：

blocks.html_en: • field - column name, JSONB path (attributes->>amount), or schema-prefixed (m.doc_type)

sentences.en: field - column name, JSONB path (attributes->>amount), or schema-prefixed (m.doc_type)
sentences.zh: 字段 - 列名称、JSONB 路径（属性->>金额）或模式前缀 (m.doc_type)

sentences.en: operator - the comparison operator (see table below)
sentences.zh: 运算符 - 比较运算符（见下表）

sentences.en: value - the value to compare against

blocks.html_en: A flat list of filters is joined with AND:

sentences.en: A flat list of filters is joined with AND:
sentences.zh: 过滤器的平面列表用 AND 连接：

blocks.html_en: Some operators don't need a value:

sentences.en: Some operators don't need a value:

sentences.zh: 一些运算符会获取一个列表：

blocks.html_en: Wrap filters in a dict with key 'or':

sentences.en: Wrap filters in a dict with key 'or':
sentences.zh: 使用键“or”将过滤器包装在字典中：

blocks.html_en: OR groups can contain AND groups and vice versa:

sentences.en: OR groups can contain AND groups and vice versa:
sentences.zh: OR 组可以包含 AND 组，反之亦然：

blocks.html_en: scope is a dict of fixed field: value pairs applied as = filters to every operation on the Datasource - select, where, update, delete, and insert.

sentences.en: scope is a dict of fixed field: value pairs applied as = filters to every operation on the Datasource - select, where, update, delete, and insert.
sentences.zh: 范围是固定字段的字典：值对作为 = 过滤器应用于数据源上的每个操作 - 选择、其中、更新、删除和插入。

blocks.html_en: Columns are passed per call to select(), not at construction time - they can be computed at runtime.

sentences.en: Columns are passed per call to select(), not at construction time - they can be computed at runtime.
sentences.zh: 列是在每次调用 select() 时传递的，而不是在构造时传递的 - 它们可以在运行时计算。

blocks.html_en: Each column is a dict with a field key. Any extra keys (label, visible, editable etc.) are ignored by filtersql and can be used by your frontend layer:

sentences.en: Each column is a dict with a field key.
sentences.zh: 每列都是一个带有字段键的字典。

sentences.en: Any extra keys (label, visible, editable etc.) are ignored by filtersql and can be used by your frontend layer:
sentences.zh: 任何额外的键（标签、可见、可编辑等）都会被filtersql忽略，并且可以被前端层使用：
普通字符串也可以工作：

sentences.en: All three return (query, values) like select().
sentences.zh: 所有三个都像 select() 一样返回（查询、值）。

blocks.html_en: where() - just the WHERE clause

sentences.zh: where() - 只是 WHERE 子句

blocks.html_en: When you want to inject filters into your own handwritten query:

sentences.en: When you want to inject filters into your own handwritten query:
sentences.zh: 当您想将过滤器注入您自己的手写查询中时：

blocks.html_en: Builds any query from a single payload dict. Useful for REST APIs and AI-generated actions:

sentences.en: Builds any query from a single payload dict.
sentences.zh: 从单个有效负载字典构建任何查询。

sentences.en: Useful for REST APIs and AI-generated actions:
sentences.zh: 对于 REST API 和 AI 生成的操作很有用：

sentences.en: Supported actions: select, insert, update, delete.
sentences.zh: 支持的操作：选择、插入、更新、删除。

blocks.html_en: Replaces placeholders with actual values for logging. Never use the output as real SQL.

sentences.en: Replaces placeholders with actual values for logging.
sentences.zh: 将占位符替换为实际记录值。

sentences.en: Never use the output as real SQL.
sentences.zh: 切勿将输出用作真正的 SQL。
人工智能/法学硕士管道

blocks.html_en: LLMs should never generate SQL directly. They should generate structured intent. filtersql validates that intent and compiles it into parameterized SQL. The values are always parameterized - no SQL injection risk even if the model produces unexpected output.

sentences.en: LLMs should never generate SQL directly.
sentences.zh: LLM 永远不应该直接生成 SQL。
他们应该产生结构化的意图。

sentences.en: filtersql validates that intent and compiles it into parameterized SQL.
sentences.zh: filtersql 验证该意图并将其编译为参数化 SQL。

sentences.en: The values are always parameterized - no SQL injection risk even if the model produces unexpected output.
sentences.zh: 这些值始终是参数化的 - 即使模型产生意外的输出，也没有 SQL 注入风险。
没有 Pydantic 的最简单版本：
使用 Pydantic 进行更严格的验证：

blocks.html_en: Without the cast, '9' > '10' as text gives wrong results for numeric ranges.

sentences.en: Without the cast, '9' > '10' as text gives wrong results for numeric ranges.
sentences.zh: 如果没有强制转换，“9”>“10”作为文本会给出错误的数字范围结果。

blocks.html_en: Navigates large tables without OFFSET - no performance degradation.

sentences.en: Navigates large tables without OFFSET - no performance degradation.
sentences.zh: 在没有 OFFSET 的情况下导航大型表 - 不会降低性能。
范围 - 值必须为 [low, high]

区分大小写的子字符串排除
区分大小写的前缀排除
不区分大小写的子字符串
不区分大小写的子字符串排除
NULL 检查 - 不需要值

sentences.en: Value in a set of columns - ?

sentences.zh: 区分大小写的正则表达式

sentences.zh: 不区分大小写的正则表达式（Pg：~*，Oracle：regexp_like with i）

sentences.en: Full-text search on indexed column (Pg, MySQL)
Full-text search on text column (Pg only)

blocks.html_en: • examples/datatables/ - Flask + DataTables server-side with column filters and global search

sentences.en: examples/datatables/ - Flask + DataTables server-side with column filters and global search
sentences.zh: example/datatables/ - Flask + DataTables 服务器端，具有列过滤器和全局搜索

blocks.html_en: • examples/pagination/ - REST API gateway with security whitelist for insert/update/delete

sentences.en: examples/pagination/ - REST API gateway with security whitelist for insert/update/delete
sentences.zh: 示例/分页/ - REST API 网关，具有用于插入/更新/删除的安全白名单

sentences.en: examples/ai/ - Gemini structured output → filtersql → SQLite
sentences.zh: 示例/ai/ - Gemini 结构化输出→filtersql→SQLite

content_html_zh:
前端、REST API 和 LLM 自然会生成结构化 JSON，而不是 SQL。 filtersql 定义了一种声明式 JSON 查询语言，并将其编译为安全的参数化 SQL。

JSON payload (or Python dicts) → filtersql → SQL string + values list

它故意不是 ORM 或连接管理器。 它构建查询并返回它。 查询执行仍然由调用者负责，这使得filtersql与任何Python DB驱动程序兼容。

专为三种用例而设计：

  • DataTables 服务器端后端
  • 基于光标的分页（Access 样式，无 OFFSET）
  • 确定性 LLM 管道 - LLM 生成 JSON，filtersql 将其编译为安全 SQL

支持 PostgreSQL、SQLite、MySQL 和 Oracle。

特征

  • 默认安全 - 完全参数化查询，防止 SQL 注入
  • 多数据库支持 - PostgreSQL、SQLite、MySQL 和 Oracle
  • 轻量级 - 不需要 ORM。 适用于任何数据库驱动程序（psycopg2、sqlite3、mysql-connector、cx_Oracle 等）
  • 与语言无关 - 干净的 JSON 协议，非常适合 REST API 和前端应用程序
  • 高性能分页 - 键集（基于光标）分页，避免缓慢的 OFFSET 查询
  • AI/LLM 友好 - 专为大型语言模型的结构化输出而设计

为什么要过滤sql？

filtersql 是故意的：

  • 无国籍的
  • 确定性的
  • 与驱动程序无关
  • 参数化的
  • 陈述性的
  • 便携的
  • JSON优先
  • 法学硕士友好

应用程序描述了他们想要什么。 filtersql决定如何用SQL表达。

例子

来自 JSON：

{
  "action": "select",
  "source": "users",
  "filters": [
    {
      "field": "name",
      "operator": "icontains",
      "value": "john"
    }
  ]
}

to SQL:

select
  *
from
  "users"
where
  "name" ilike '%' || ? || '%'

具有值：

["john"]

安装

pip install filtersql

快速启动

import filtersql

ds = filtersql.Datasource(
    source      = 'users',
    dbms        = 'Pg',
    placeholder = '%s',
)

query, values = ds.select(
    columns = [
        {'field': 'id'},
        {'field': 'first_name'},
        {'field': 'last_name'},
    ],
    filters = [
        {'field': 'first_name', 'operator': 'icontains', 'value': 'John'},
        {'field': 'last_name',  'operator': 'icontains', 'value': 'Smith'},
    ],
    order = [{'field': 'first_name', 'order': 'asc'}],
    limit = {'start': 0, 'length': 10},
)

print(query)
# select
#   "id",
#   "first_name",
#   "last_name"
# from
#   "users"
# where
#   ("first_name" ilike '%' || %s || '%' and "last_name" ilike '%' || %s || '%')
# order by
#   "first_name" asc
# limit 10 offset 0

print(values)
# ['John', 'Smith']

cursor.execute(query, values)
rows = cursor.fetchall()

过滤器

过滤器格式

每个过滤器都是一个具有三个键的字典：

{'field': 'status', 'operator': '=', 'value': 'active'}
  • 字段 - 列名称、JSONB 路径（属性->>金额）或模式前缀 (m.doc_type)
  • 运算符 - 比较运算符（见下表）
  • value - 要比较的值

过滤器的平面列表用 AND 连接：

filters = [
    {'field': 'status',   'operator': '=',         'value': 'active'},
    {'field': 'doc_date', 'operator': '>=',        'value': '2025-01-01'},
    {'field': 'title',    'operator': 'icontains', 'value': 'oxygen'},
]

有些运算符不需要值：

{'field': 'deleted_at', 'operator': 'null'}
{'field': 'deleted_at', 'operator': 'notnull'}

一些运算符会获取一个列表：

{'field': 'status',   'operator': 'in',      'value': ['active', 'pending']}
{'field': 'doc_date', 'operator': 'between', 'value': ['2025-01-01', '2025-12-31']}

或组

使用键“or”将过滤器包装在字典中：

filters = [
    {'field': 'status', 'operator': '=', 'value': 'active'},
    {'or': [
        {'field': 'first_name', 'operator': 'icontains', 'value': 'john'},
        {'field': 'last_name',  'operator': 'icontains', 'value': 'john'},
    ]},
]
# → "status" = ? AND ("first_name" like '%'||?||'%' OR "last_name" like '%'||?||'%')

嵌套

OR 组可以包含 AND 组，反之亦然：

filters = [
    {'or': [
        {'field': 'doc_type', 'operator': '=', 'value': 'CONTRACT'},
        {'and': [
            {'field': 'doc_type', 'operator': '=',  'value': 'ORDER'},
            {'field': 'amount',   'operator': '>=', 'value': '10000'},
        ]},
    ]},
]
# → ("doc_type" = ? OR ("doc_type" = ? AND "amount" >= ?))

scope

范围是固定字段的字典：值对作为 = 过滤器应用于数据源上的每个操作 - 选择、其中、更新、删除和插入。

ds = filtersql.Datasource(
    source      = 'documents',
    dbms        = 'Pg',
    placeholder = '%s',
    scope       = {'tenant_id': 42},
)

# scope is always added
query, values = ds.select(columns=columns, filters=user_filters)
# → ... WHERE "tenant_id" = %s AND ...

query, values = ds.delete(id={'id': 99})
# → ... WHERE "id" = %s AND "tenant_id" = %s

query, values = ds.insert(values={'title': 'New doc'})
# → INSERT INTO "documents" ("title", "tenant_id") VALUES (%s, %s)

专栏

列是在每次调用 select() 时传递的，而不是在构造时传递的 - 它们可以在运行时计算。

每列都是一个带有字段键的字典。 任何额外的键（标签、可见、可编辑等）都会被filtersql忽略，并且可以被前端层使用：

columns = [
    {'field': 'id',         'label': 'ID',         'visible': True,  'editable': False},
    {'field': 'first_name', 'label': 'First Name', 'visible': True,  'editable': True},
    {'field': 'last_name',  'label': 'Last Name',  'visible': True,  'editable': True},
]

普通字符串也可以工作：

columns = ['id', 'first_name', 'last_name']

raw=True 的原始表达式：

columns = [
    {'field': 'COUNT(*) as total', 'raw': True},
    {'field': 'MAX(age) as max_age', 'raw': True},
]

插入、更新、删除

所有三个都像 select() 一样返回（查询、值）。

# insert
query, values = ds.insert(
    values = {'first_name': 'John', 'last_name': 'Smith'},
)

# insert with returning (PostgreSQL only)
query, values = ds.insert(
    values    = {'first_name': 'John'},
    returning = 'id',
)

# update - id can be single or composite key
query, values = ds.update(
    id     = {'id': 42},
    values = {'first_name': 'John', 'status': 'active'},
)
# values order: SET values first, then WHERE values
# → ['John', 'active', 42]

# delete
query, values = ds.delete(id={'id': 42})

where() - 只是 WHERE 子句

当您想将过滤器注入您自己的手写查询中时：

ds = filtersql.Datasource(source='documents', dbms='Pg', placeholder='%s')

where_clause, values = ds.where(filters=[
    {'field': 'doc_type', 'operator': '=',  'value': 'CONTRACT'},
    {'field': 'doc_date', 'operator': '>=', 'value': '2025-01-01'},
])
# → '("doc_type" = %s and "doc_date" >= %s)'
# → ['CONTRACT', '2025-01-01']

query = f"""
    SELECT v.chunk, m.doc_type
    FROM file_vectors v
    JOIN file_metadata m ON v.sha256 = m.sha256
    WHERE v.model_name = %s
    AND {where_clause}
    ORDER BY v.embedding <=> %s
"""

filtersql() - 便利函数

从单个有效负载字典构建任何查询。 对于 REST API 和 AI 生成的操作很有用：

from filtersql import filtersql

query, values = filtersql(
    payload = {
        'action':  'select',
        'source':  'users',
        'columns': [{'field': 'id'}, {'field': 'first_name'}],
        'filters': [{'field': 'status', 'operator': '=', 'value': 'active'}],
        'order':   [{'field': 'id', 'order': 'asc'}],
        'limit':   {'start': 0, 'length': 10},
    },
    dbms        = 'Pg',
    placeholder = '%s',
)

支持的操作：选择、插入、更新、删除。

debug()

将占位符替换为实际记录值。 切勿将输出用作真正的 SQL。

query, values = ds.select(columns=columns, filters=filters)
print(ds.debug(query, values))
# select
#   "id",
#   "first_name"
# from
#   "users"
# where
#   ("first_name" ilike '%' || 'John' || '%')

人工智能/法学硕士管道

LLM 永远不应该直接生成 SQL。 他们应该产生结构化的意图。 filtersql 验证该意图并将其编译为参数化 SQL。 这些值始终是参数化的 - 即使模型产生意外的输出，也没有 SQL 注入风险。

user question → LLM → JSON → filtersql → parameterized SQL

# DANGEROUS!
# the naive approach everyone starts with
query = f"SELECT * FROM users WHERE {llm_output}"  # SQL injection waiting to happen

# with filtersql
payload = json.loads(llm_response)
query, values = filtersql(payload, dbms='Pg', placeholder='%s')
cursor.execute(query, values)  # always parameterized, always safe

没有 Pydantic 的最简单版本：

import json
from google import genai
from filtersql import filtersql

SCHEMA = {
    "type": "OBJECT",
    "properties": {
        "source": {"type": "STRING", "enum": ["users", "contracts"]},
        "filters": {
            "type": "ARRAY",
            "items": {
                "type": "OBJECT",
                "properties": {
                    "field":    {"type": "STRING"},
                    "operator": {"type": "STRING", "enum": ["=", "!=", ">", ">=", "<", "<=", "icontains", "in"]},
                    "value":    {"type": "STRING"}
                },
                "required": ["field", "operator", "value"]
            }
        }
    },
    "required": ["source", "filters"]
}

response = client.models.generate_content(
    model='gemini-flash',
    contents=user_question,
    config=genai.types.GenerateContentConfig(
        system_instruction="Convert user questions into SQL filter payloads.",
        response_mime_type="application/json",
        response_schema=SCHEMA,
        temperature=0.0,
    )
)

payload = json.loads(response.text)
query, values = filtersql(payload, action='select', dbms='SQLite', placeholder='?')

使用 Pydantic 进行更严格的验证：

from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from typing import List, Literal

class SQLFilter(BaseModel):
    field: Literal['doc_type', 'doc_date', 'author', 'title']
    operator: Literal['=', '!=', '>', '>=', '<', '<=', 'icontains', 'between', 'in', 'notin']
    value: str

class QuerySchema(BaseModel):
    semantic_query: str
    sql_filters: List[SQLFilter]

parsed = QuerySchema.model_validate_json(response.text)
filters = [f.model_dump() for f in parsed.sql_filters]

ds = filtersql.Datasource(source='documents', dbms='Pg', placeholder='%s')
where_clause, values = ds.where(filters=filters)

JSONB 和 value_type (PostgreSQL)

{'field': 'attributes->>amount',  'operator': '>=', 'value': '10000',     'value_type': 'numeric'}
# → ("attributes"->>'amount')::numeric >= %s::numeric

{'field': 'attributes->>expiry',  'operator': '<=', 'value': '2025-12-31', 'value_type': 'date'}
# → ("attributes"->>'expiry')::date <= %s::date

如果没有强制转换，“9”>“10”作为文本会给出错误的数字范围结果。

基于光标的分页

在没有 OFFSET 的情况下导航大型表 - 不会降低性能。

ds = filtersql.Datasource(
    source = 'documents',
    dbms   = 'Pg',
    order  = [{'field': 'id', 'order': 'asc'}],
)

# forward
query, values = ds.select(
    columns   = columns,
    cursor    = {'id': last_seen_id},
    direction = 'next',
)

# backward
query, values = ds.select(
    columns   = columns,
    cursor    = {'id': last_seen_id},
    direction = 'prev',
)

# jump to specific record
query, values = ds.select(
    columns   = columns,
    cursor    = {'id': 42},
    direction = 'seek',
)
方向 SQL 使用案例
'next' 字段 > 最后一个值 向前导航
'prev' 字段 < 最后一个值 向后导航
'seek' 字段=值 跳转到特定记录

多列光标也可以工作：

query, values = ds.select(
    columns   = columns,
    cursor    = {'first_name': last_first, 'last_name': last_last},
    direction = 'next',
)

全文检索

PostgreSQL

# pre-built tsvector column
{'field': 'tsv_content', 'operator': 'fts', 'value': 'oxygen supply'}
# → "tsv_content" @@ websearch_to_tsquery('english', %s)

# on the fly from a text column
{'field': 'description', 'operator': 'fts_query', 'value': 'oxygen supply'}
# → to_tsvector('english', coalesce("description", '')) @@ websearch_to_tsquery('english', %s)

ds = filtersql.Datasource(..., fts_language='italian')

MySQL

{'field': 'content', 'operator': 'fts', 'value': 'oxygen supply'}
# → match("content") against(? in boolean mode)

过滤运算符

操作员 描述
= != > >= < <= 比较
之间 范围 - 值必须为 [low, high]
包含 区分大小写的子字符串
开始于 区分大小写的前缀
结束于 区分大小写的后缀
不包含 区分大小写的子字符串排除
不开始于 区分大小写的前缀排除
图标包含 不区分大小写的子字符串
istarts_with 不区分大小写的前缀
朋友与 不区分大小写的后缀
不包含图标 不区分大小写的子字符串排除
空/非空 NULL 检查 - 不需要值
游泳/游泳 列出会员资格
反向输入 一组列中的值 - ? IN（第 1 列，第 2 列）
正则表达式 区分大小写的正则表达式
正则表达式 不区分大小写的正则表达式（Pg：~*，Oracle：regexp_like with i）
fts 索引列上的全文搜索（Pg、MySQL）
fts_查询 文本列上的全文搜索（仅限 Pg）

支持的数据库

DBMS 数据库管理系统值 默认占位符
PostgreSQL 'Pg' %s
SQLite 'SQLite' ?
MySQL 'mysql' ?
甲骨文 ‘甲骨文’ ?

使用驱动程序期望的任何占位符覆盖：

ds = filtersql.Datasource(..., placeholder='%s')
ds = filtersql.Datasource(..., placeholder=':val')

示例

工作示例位于 /examples 文件夹中：

  • example/datatables/ - Flask + DataTables 服务器端，具有列过滤器和全局搜索
  • 示例/分页/ - REST API 网关，具有用于插入/更新/删除的安全白名单
  • 示例/ai/ - Gemini 结构化输出→filtersql→SQLite
